title: Piquant Charred Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Algerian dish featuring charred lamb, white wine, and a nimono-simmer, perfect for a brunch mood, this recipe is also diabetic friendly due to the use of low carb ingredients and portion control.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium high heat.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve the tagine hot.
Serve with a side of couscous or roasted vegetables for a filling meal.
